Operationally, you need to define what success is and you need to set those expectations consistently in business. I have both these words written down because I meant it in the sentence that I just said with expectations being success, but it's what you also got to understand. Expectations rely internally and externally completely on you to constantly calibrate them for success. Here's a little bit of what I mean. You go to a client and they say, you know, you're giving me all this stuff really excited for the Huffington article you have coming out. You do have an expectation set that they are expecting a Huffington Post article and that's what they're excited about. Out of everything else that you can do for them, everything else should fall below the priority of what they are expecting in order to dictate the success and that's what they were lying on. Take that sentence in one more time. You have clients tell you all the time, hey, I'm so excited for this because it's going to give me that. All this other stuff is great too. I'm really excited for that. That's what you want to focus on. That's the expectation for what they have success as in their mind. Even though everything that you know you need to accomplish in order to generate technical success to you, success to them is what matters because their success in their mind and them having success through the relationship with you is going to dictate if you get the recurring relationship for your next paycheck or not. Understand that your ability to continue understanding and calibrating in real time have you accomplish those expectations they have to find the next ones to continue calibrating success. Expectations are a success. The same thing goes internally. Internally you need to go to your staff members all the time and you need to say, what is your version of success with working for me? What is it that you're seeking out of this relationship with being a staff member with me? Are you looking for mentorship, revenue, are you looking for long term potential, are you looking for management? What can I do to help you? Having those questions asked dictates the success of you being able to not have a high attrition rate, retain good staff members and have your staff really fuck with you to really show that you care for these people. Because you should! That being said, expectations are success internally as well for you to be able to go to your team and say, hey, this is what I'm expecting. This is success to me on handling this client relationship. You guys understand my intentions and what my expectations are successful. They say yes, they know, accept the mission and you can now hold them accountable as we spoke about in the previous video to get whatever it is you need done in timetable you need it done because they've accepted the expectations of what success are. Don't deviate from this logic. It's a very simple idea and it's a very simple video. Your ability to consistently recalibrate your expectations, your client's expectations and your team's expectations towards what their current version of success is because their historical version of success may have been met, it may be dynamic and it may have changed or it just may not exist anymore, it may not matter to the person. Your consistent ability to calibrate for expectations on what the new version of success is for yourself, for your clients, for your team, more importantly from both angles of your team and you, it's going to dictate your success. This is phase 5 operations and this has been the expectations are success.
